correct_text:
Court Name Or Jurisdiction: The court where the case is filed (e.g., United States District Court for the Northern District of California).
Case Type Or Docket: The case number or docket identifier assigned by the court, sometimes shown as a docket number or civil/criminal designation.
Parties’ Names: The plaintiff and defendant—or petitioners and respondents in appellate matters.
Case Caption Line: A single line that succinctly describes the nature of the action, such as “John A. Doe, Plaintiff, v. Jane B. Smith, Defendant.”
Jurisdictional Designation: Indication of the case’s nature (e.g., civil, criminal, family, probate) when required by the court rules.
Attorney Information (Optional On Caption): In some jurisdictions, the caption may include attorney names and contact details on the first page or in a separate cover sheet.
